mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate suppresses the equity risk-free discount rate, keeping present-value valuations elevated and producing a positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1%.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.15 at t-1mo indicates zero-rate policy
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically greater than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline of at least 0.3 percentage points in UNRATE signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises expected consumer income and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by >= 0.3pp over the trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=9.8 at t-1mo signals elevated slack with potential improvement trajectory
failure_condition: Triggered instances show 20-day forward positive-return hit rate below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal broad-based consumer and investment demand expansion; neither series alone is sufficient, but their co-movement compounds to predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, since demand strength must appear across both durable and non-durable channels to be a reliable macro signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows no statistically significant excess return relative to single-series triggers, or interaction term coefficient is not significant at p < 0.10 in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals deteriorating supply-side momentum and rising recession probability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as equity risk premia widen in anticipation of earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered contraction episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth predicts S&P 500 forward returns with opposite sign depending on the macro regime: in a post-recession recovery with elevated UNRATE, strong payroll gains signal genuine demand restoration and are bullish, whereas in a late-cycle expansion with low UNRATE, strong payroll gains signal labor-cost pressure and are bearish for equities over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=9.8 indicates recovery-phase regime rather than late-cycle tightness
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with UNRATE above 8%
failure_condition: Direction of the PAYEMS-return relationship does not flip when UNRATE crosses below 6% in out-of-sample data, or Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero (|IC| < 0.02) within the recovery regime subsample.

mechanism_narrative: The real short rate, approximated by the gap between FEDFUNDS and trailing CPIAUCSL inflation, drives S&P 500 forward returns: when nominal rates are near zero but CPI inflation is positive, the resulting deeply negative real rate incentivizes risk-asset allocation, producing positive returns over a quarterly horizon. The effect requires both series jointly, since FEDFUNDS alone at zero is uninformative without confirming CPI trend.
failure_condition: Negative-real-rate triggered periods do not outperform the unconditional 60-day return distribution at p < 0.10, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broad economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon regardless of short-term market conditions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth quintiles show no monotonic relationship with subsequent 60-day SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across multiple 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical threshold signals an abrupt pullback in construction activity and consumer confidence, producing a short-lived negative S&P 500 return reaction over the following week as markets price in weaker forward demand.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST drops exceeding 10% are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across a 3-year walk-forward sample, or hit rate for negative returns falls below 50%.
